decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs
decisions.detail: The authentication module was shipped last Friday with no P0 bugs reported.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry is currently set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails are not rate-limited
decisions.detail: Carol flagged that password reset emails are currently not subject to rate-limiting.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Max 3 password reset attempts per hour
decisions.detail: It was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28 that password reset attempts should be limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API gateway to use AWS API Gateway
decisions.detail: The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway

decisions.title: API rate limit of 100 req/min per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on Ben's recommendation from load tests.

evidence.quote: with a 100 req/min limit per user. This was Ben's recommendation based on load tests.

decisions.title: Auth API spec target publish date is 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Explicit decision: Auth API spec publish target date is 2026-07-05

decisions.title: Open question: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down?

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Open question: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?
decisions.detail: A question remains regarding whether refresh tokens are automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate-limiting for password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour.
Configure AWS API Gateway to enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Develop and publish the Authentication API specification.
Implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry must be set to 24 hours.
Password reset attempts must be limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.
The API gateway must enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05 to unblock the mobile app team.
Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must be in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.
JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?
Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully logs in, When a JWT token is issued, Then its validity period SHALL be 24 hours from the time of issuance.
Given a valid JWT token, When more than 24 hours have passed since its issuance, Then API requests using this token SHALL be rejected.

Given a user attempts to reset their password, When fewer than 3 password reset requests have been made by that user within the last 60 minutes, Then a password reset email SHALL be successfully sent.

Given a user attempts to reset their password, When 3 password reset requests have already been made by that user within the last 60 minutes, Then the password reset request SHALL be rejected, and no additional email SHALL be sent.

Given a user's password reset attempts have been rate-limited, When 60 minutes have elapsed since the first attempt in the rate-limited window, Then the user SHALL be able to request another password reset.
Given an authenticated user, When the user makes up to 100 API requests within a 60-second period, Then all requests SHALL be successfully processed.
Given an authenticated user, When the user attempts to make the 101st API request within a 60-second period, Then the request SHALL be rejected with a rate-limiting error.

Given an authenticated user's requests have been rate-limited, When 60 seconds have elapsed since the first request in the rate-limited window, Then the user SHALL be able to make subsequent API requests.
Given Sprint 3 is ready for sign-off, When checking the authentication module, Then automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset endpoints SHALL be implemented and passing.
test_scenarios: Verify a successful API call immediately after login with a fresh token.
Attempt an API call at 23 hours 59 minutes after token issuance and verify success.
Attempt an API call at 24 hours 1 minute after token issuance and verify rejection.
Attempt password reset 3 times successfully within a 59-minute window.
Attempt password reset a 4th time within the same 59-minute window and verify rejection.
Attempt password reset after 3 prior attempts, waiting more than 60 minutes from the first attempt, and verify success.
Perform 100 API requests within 50 seconds and verify all succeed.
Perform 101 API requests within 50 seconds and verify the 101st request is rejected with a rate-limiting error.
Perform 101 API requests within 50 seconds, verify rejection, then wait 65 seconds and attempt another request, verifying success.
Verify the existence and passing status of automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
edge_cases: Attempt to use a JWT token exactly at the 24-hour mark (boundary condition) to verify expiry behavior.
Initiate concurrent password reset requests from the same user close to the 3 attempts/hour limit.
Perform password reset attempts spanning the exact 60-minute boundary (e.g., 3 attempts at minute 59, then 1 attempt at minute 60 of the new window).
Initiate concurrent API requests from the same user hitting the 100 requests/minute limit.
Perform API requests spanning the exact 60-second boundary to verify rate limit resets.
Test multiple distinct users simultaneously hitting their individual 100 requests/minute rate limits.
Validate the specific error message returned when password reset attempts are rate-limited.
Validate the specific error message returned when API requests are rate-limited.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes inoperable?
Are refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset?

decision_history: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs reported.
It was confirmed that the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, reiterating a previous Sprint 2 agreement.
A decision was made in the security review on 2026-06-28 to limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour.
It was decided that the API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway, and it will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on load test recommendations.
It was requested and agreed that all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.
risks: Password reset emails are currently not subject to rate-limiting, which poses a potential security vulnerability (likelihood: confirmed, impact: high).
open_questions: Are refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset? (Impact: High - security and user experience)
milestones: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs (last Friday)
Automated integration tests for all auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) completed before Sprint 3 sign-off

system_requirements: The authentication module SHALL issue JWT tokens with an expiry of 24 hours.
The authentication module SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour per user.
The system SHALL implement rate limiting for password reset requests to prevent abuse (inferred — needs confirmation on specific mechanism).
The API gateway SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The system SHALL have automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: User authentication requests (e.g., login, refresh, password reset) SHALL flow through the AWS API Gateway to the Authentication Module.
JWT tokens SHALL be issued by the Authentication Module to client applications (e.g., Mobile App) upon successful authentication or token refresh.
Password reset requests SHALL trigger an email notification containing a reset link or code to the user (inferred — needs confirmation on mechanism).
interface_contracts: The Authentication API specification, detailing endpoints for login, token refresh, and password reset, SHALL be formally defined and published.
JWT tokens issued by the Authentication Module SHALL include an expiry claim ('exp') set for 24 hours from issuance.
The password reset API endpoint SHALL enforce a server-side rate limit of 3 attempts per hour per user, identifiable by a unique user identifier (e.g., IP address, user ID, email address).
All API endpoints exposed via AWS API Gateway SHALL be subject to a global rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on the requesting user's identity.

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway implementation SHALL utilize AWS API Gateway.
The JWT token expiry SHALL be configured for 24 hours.
The system SHALL implement a password reset rate limit of 3 attempts per hour.
The API Gateway SHALL enforce a user-based rate limit of 100 requests per minute.

open_analysis_questions: What is the required fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes inoperable?
Are refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset?
Does the password reset rate limit of 3 per hour apply to the request for the reset email, or the actual attempt to reset the password after receiving the email, or both?
Is the API rate limit of 100 req/min per user applied universally across all API endpoints exposed via the gateway, or only to specific authentication-related endpoints?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user authentication system has been successfully completed with no critical issues. However, the mobile app development team is currently blocked and cannot proceed until the technical details for this new authentication system are officially published by July 5th. We have established several key security measures and identified an immediate risk regarding password reset emails that requires attention.
key_impact: The new user login system is stable and ready for integration, providing reliable access for users.
The mobile app's launch timeline is at risk as the development team is blocked from integrating the login system until the authentication specification is published by July 5th.
Important security features have been implemented to protect user accounts, such as limiting password reset attempts to three per hour and session validity to 24 hours.
A critical security vulnerability exists because password reset emails are currently not limited, which could be exploited and needs to be addressed urgently.
major_unresolved_questions: What is the plan for keeping our services running smoothly if the main system handling all user requests (API gateway) experiences an outage, and how will this affect users?
When a user resets their password, will they be automatically logged out of all other devices to ensure maximum security?
We need to urgently implement a solution to limit the number of password reset emails a user can request to prevent potential abuse.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. There are no critical issues or hallucinations. The outputs are well-structured and tailored to their respective audiences. Inferred needs for confirmation are correctly flagged by the Systems Analysis view. The Stakeholder view successfully rephrases technical questions for a business audience without introducing new facts. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.
